content.content:
1	import type { ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";
2	
3	// Tagline sourced from src/content/projects/hivemind/thesis.ts.
4	// Framing composed from thesis.ts (mechanism), capabilities.ts (CAP list),
5	// workflow.ts (six-step pipeline), and the layered scoring contract in
6	// src/lib/coherenceEngine/arguments.ts (CE2 §2-§4).
7	export const metadata: ProjectMetadata = {
8	  slug: "hivemind",
9	  code: "HVM",
10	  title: "Hivemind",
11	  tagline: "Strategic analytical software.",
12	  status: "in-progress",
13	  framing:
14	    "Hivemind decomposes strategy questions into evaluable hypotheses, attaches weighted evidence, and aggregates probabilistic posteriors into a structured brief. The output is a structured memo with citations and confidence intervals, not a chat transcript. The bet is that decision-grade analysis benefits from explicit structure scored against a layered coherence contract.",
15	  externalLinks: [
16	    { label: "hivemind.ai", href: "https://hivemind.ai" },
17	    { label: "app.hivemind.ai", href: "https://app.hivemind.ai" },
18	  ],
19	  seedQuestion: "what does Hivemind actually do?",
20	};
21	
22	export default metadata;
23

file.content:
{
  "name": "personalwebsite",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "packageManager": "npm@10.9.2",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "postbuild": "next-sitemap --config next-sitemap.config.js && npm run build:resume-pdf",
    "build:resume-pdf": "tsx scripts/resume/build-pdf.ts",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@mdx-js/loader": "^3.1.1",
    "@mdx-js/react": "^3.1.1",
    "@next/mdx": "^16.2.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.1.15",
    "@radix-ui/react-visually-hidden": "^1.2.4",
    "@types/mdx": "^2.0.13",
    "@vercel/analytics": "^2.0.1",
    "framer-motion": "^12.38.0",
    "fuse.js": "^7.3.0",
    "next": "^15.1.0",
    "next-sitemap": "^4.2.3",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "zod": "^4.3.6",
    "zustand": "^5.0.12"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@axe-core/react": "^4.11.2",
    "@playwright/test": "^1.59.1",
    "@testing-library/react": "^16.3.2",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "axe-core": "^4.11.3",
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "^15.1.0",
    "jsdom": "^29.0.2",
    "prettier": "^3.8.3",
    "stylelint": "^17.8.0",
    "stylelint-config-standard": "^40.0.0",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0",
    "vitest": "^4.1.4"
  }
}

content.content:
1	import type { ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";
2	
3	// Tagline + framing sourced from existing repo content:
4	//   - thesis.ts      → claim ("philosophical debate can be systematized"),
5	//                       method (extract first principles, graph supports/
6	//                       tensions, surface contradictions), role
7	//                       ("a Kim/Codex for the operator-philosopher")
8	//   - methodology.ts → extraction with provenance, Noosphere walking the
9	//                       graph, slow structured collaborator (not a chatbot)
10	//   - principles.ts  → the seed dataset (P-001..P-080) with explicit
11	//                       supports / tensions edges
12	// The intended src/lib/theseus/* files (ordersDeck.ts, thesisAxes.ts,
13	// sources.ts) referenced by the prompt do not currently exist in the repo;
14	// the framing therefore stays grounded in the content/ files above.
15	export const metadata: ProjectMetadata = {
16	  slug: "theseus",
17	  code: "THS",
18	  title: "Theseus",
19	  tagline:
20	    "A knowledge system for monitoring contradiction in the principles you have already committed to.",
21	  status: "shipped",
22	  framing:
23	    "Theseus extracts first principles from discussion and writing, links them into a graph of supports and tensions, and runs a second model — Noosphere — across the graph to flag contradictions, ask refining questions, and propose edits. The product is a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves. The output is a contradiction map with provenance, not a debate.",
24	  externalLinks: [
25	    { label: "thesescodex.com", href: "https://thesescodex.com" },
26	  ],
27	  seedQuestion: "what does the Theseus project argue about identity?",
28	};
29	
30	export default metadata;
31

1	import type { ProjectMetadata } from "@/lib/projects/types";
2	
3	// Tagline + framing sourced from existing repo content:
4	//   - preface.ts / preface.mdx → severance of efficiency from purpose,
5	//     metric-as-meaning, "you have already noticed the symptoms"
6	//   - pillars.ts (I–V)        → corporatism, gamification, incumbency,
7	//                                complacency, economic revolution
8	//   - arc.ts                  → Book I of a five-volume series
9	//   - quotes.ts               → "Efficiency, severed from purpose, becomes
10	//                                the most efficient way of arriving nowhere."
11	export const metadata: ProjectMetadata = {
12	  slug: "purposeless-efficiency",
13	  code: "PRP",
14	  title: "Purposeless Efficiency",
15	  tagline: "Book I — efficiency severed from purpose.",
16	  status: "in-progress",
17	  framing:
18	    "Purposeless Efficiency is a book-length argument that the late industrial era has been reorganized around optimization without an examined end — efficiency severed from purpose, the metric become the meaning. Book I of a five-volume series, it traces the consequences across five pillars: corporatism, gamification, incumbency, complacency, and the shape of an economic revolution that does not repeat the failure modes of the last one.",
19	  externalLinks: [],
20	  seedQuestion: "what is purposeless efficiency?",
21	};
22	
23	export default metadata;
24

content.content:
preserved; only the lightness was lifted.

### Pairs that pass without adjustment
All other `fg`/`bg` and accent pairs cleared AA at the prompt's
specified values:

| pair                    | dark ratio | light ratio |
|-------------------------|------------|-------------|
| `fg` / `bg`             | 15.50      | 16.68       |
| `fg` / `bg-mute`        | 14.42      | 15.16       |
| `fg` / `bg-raise`       | 13.29      | 17.40       |
| `fg-mute` / `bg`        | 5.59       | 7.19        |
| `fg-mute` / `bg-mute`   | 5.20       | 6.53        |
| `fg-mute` / `bg-raise`  | 4.79       | 7.50        |
| `accent` / `bg`         | 8.64       | 4.82        |
| `accent` / `bg-mute`    | 8.04       | 4.38¹       |
| `accent` / `bg-raise`   | 7.41       | 5.03        |
| `accent-fg` / `accent`  | 8.64       | 4.82        |
| `warn` / `bg` (post-fix)| 6.87       | 4.84        |
| `error` / `bg` (post-fix)| 5.96      | 6.10        |
| `ok` / `bg`             | 6.41       | 5.75        |

¹ `accent` on `--bg-mute` in light theme is 4.38 — below 4.5 for
normal text but above 3:1 for large text. Accent text is reserved
for accent links and the palette caret (large mono), so AA-large
suffices. If a future component renders accent-coloured normal text
on `--bg-mute`, switch to `--bg` (4.82) or `--bg-raise` (5.03).


## Mobile parity — touch-target checklist (suite 16/P09)

WCAG 2.5.5 (Target Size, AAA) — every standalone interactive control
on a project page must measure ≥ 44 × 44 CSS px when the viewport is
< 640px wide. Inline links inside `.p-prose` are exempt under the
inline-text exception (2.5.5).

| Element                                 | Selector / file                                                   | Status |
|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------|
| Standalone anchor / `Link` (within shell) | `article.ps-shell a.p-link`, `header.ph-hero a.p-link`             | ≥ 44×44 — enforced via `prose.css` `@media (max-width: 639px)` |
| `Button` (within shell)                  | `article.ps-shell .p-btn`, `article.ps-shell button`, `header.ph-hero button` | ≥ 44×44 — enforced via `prose.css` `@media (max-width: 639px)` |
| Anchor copy-link affordance              | `.p-anchor__copy`                                                  | Hover-only on desktop; **always visible** at < 640px (B3) |
| Aside callouts                           | `.p-aside`                                                         | Full-bleed via negative `margin-inline`, internal `padding` (B4) |
| Container padding                        | `article.ps-shell`, `header.ph-hero`                               | `padding-inline: var(--s-4)` at < 640px (B1) |
| Section vertical gap                     | `.ps-shell-sections`                                               | `gap: var(--s-7)` desktop → `var(--s-6)` at < 640px (B1) |
| Hero title scale                         | `.ph-hero__title`                                                  | `--t-3xl-size` desktop → `--t-2xl-size` at < 640px (B1) |
| Prose measure                            | `.p-prose`                                                         | 64ch desktop → 100% at < 640px (rely on container padding) |

Verification: `e2e/projects-mobile.spec.ts` exercises a 375px viewport
across all three project routes and asserts the computed CSS for the
container, section gap, hero title, and that touch targets meet 44×44.

| `fg-mute` / `bg-raise`  | 4.79       | 7.50        |
| `accent` / `bg`         | 8.64       | 4.82        |
| `accent` / `bg-mute`    | 8.04       | 4.38¹       |
| `accent` / `bg-raise`   | 7.41       | 5.03        |
| `accent-fg` / `accent`  | 8.64       | 4.82        |
| `warn` / `bg` (post-fix)| 6.87       | 4.84        |
| `error` / `bg` (post-fix)| 5.96      | 6.10        |
| `ok` / `bg`             | 6.41       | 5.75        |

¹ `accent` on `--bg-mute` in light theme is 4.38 — below 4.5 for
normal text but above 3:1 for large text. Accent text is reserved
for accent links and the palette caret (large mono), so AA-large
suffices. If a future component renders accent-coloured normal text
on `--bg-mute`, switch to `--bg` (4.82) or `--bg-raise` (5.03).


## Mobile parity — touch-target checklist (suite 16/P09)

WCAG 2.5.5 (Target Size, AAA) — every standalone interactive control
on a project page must measure ≥ 44 × 44 CSS px when the viewport is
< 640px wide. Inline links inside `.p-prose` are exempt under the
inline-text exception (2.5.5).

| Element                                 | Selector / file                                                   | Status |
|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------|
| Standalone anchor / `Link` (within shell) | `article.ps-shell a.p-link`, `header.ph-hero a.p-link`             | ≥ 44×44 — enforced via `prose.css` `@media (max-width: 639px)` |
| `Button` (within shell)                  | `article.ps-shell .p-btn`, `article.ps-shell button`, `header.ph-hero button` | ≥ 44×44 — enforced via `prose.css` `@media (max-width: 639px)` |
| Anchor copy-link affordance              | `.p-anchor__copy`                                                  | Hover-only on desktop; **always visible** at < 640px (B3) |
| Aside callouts                           | `.p-aside`                                                         | Full-bleed via negative `margin-inline`, internal `padding` (B4) |
| Container padding                        | `article.ps-shell`, `header.ph-hero`                               | `padding-inline: var(--s-4)` at < 640px (B1) |
| Section vertical gap                     | `.ps-shell-sections`                                               | `gap: var(--s-7)` desktop → `var(--s-6)` at < 640px (B1) |
| Hero title scale                         | `.ph-hero__title`                                                  | `--t-3xl-size` desktop → `--t-2xl-size` at < 640px (B1) |
| Prose measure                            | `.p-prose`                                                         | 64ch desktop → 100% at < 640px (rely on container padding) |

Verification: `e2e/projects-mobile.spec.ts` exercises a 375px viewport
across all three project routes and asserts the computed CSS for the
container, section gap, hero title, and that touch targets meet 44×44.

content.text: Now let me update the ProjectShell to add the footer CTA, and check all current state:

content.content:
1	// Prose narrative for the PRP project page.
2	// Sources (every claim grounded in existing repo content):
3	//   - preface.ts / preface.mdx → the refusal, the confession, "metric is the
4	//                                meaning", "the symptoms are not accidental"
5	//   - pillars.ts (I–V)         → corporatism, gamification, incumbency,
6	//                                 complacency, economic revolution
7	//   - arc.ts                   → five-volume arc; Book I status: writing
8	//   - progress.ts              → 92,143 / 100,000 words, manuscript v4
9	//   - quotes.ts                → severance, leaderboard-as-workflow,
10	//                                 incumbency wired into the substrate
11	// Per v2 principle 2 (content first), each section opens with its point.
12	
13	export type BodySection = {
14	  slug: string;
15	  heading: string;
16	  paragraphs: string[];
17	};
18	
19	const sections: BodySection[] = [
20	  {
21	    slug: "the-distinction",
22	    heading: "The distinction",
23	    paragraphs: [
24	      "Purposeless Efficiency is the name for a specific severance: efficiency cut loose from any external purpose, until the metric is the meaning. The book’s opening line is the cleanest statement of the argument — efficiency, severed from purpose, becomes the most efficient way of arriving nowhere. The distinction is not between efficient and inefficient systems. It is between systems that still remember what they are for and systems that have replaced their purpose with the measurement of themselves.",
25	      "The book’s claim is that this severance is not a local pathology of one industry or one institution. It is the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and the symptoms — the sense that the levers no longer connect to outcomes, the sense that the leaderboard has eaten the work — are not accidental.",
26	    ],
27	  },
28	  {
29	    slug: "why-it-matters",
30	    heading: "Why it matters",
31	    paragraphs: [
32	      "The reader, the preface assumes, has already noticed the symptoms. The book is written for the reader who has stopped pretending they are accidental. The wager is that naming the severance precisely — distinguishing optimization from purpose, metric from meaning — is a precondition for any honest response to it. Critique that misdescribes the disease prescribes the wrong medicine.",
33	      "The stakes are not aesthetic. When every workflow becomes a leaderboard, the leaderboard becomes the workflow, and the optimization target eats the optimized object. The institution survives; the thing it was supposed to do does not. Ambition, under these conditions, becomes the willingness to mistake the institution’s survival for one’s own. That substitution is what the book is trying to make visible enough to refuse.",
34	    ],
35	  },
36	  {
37	    slug: "where-it-shows-up",
38	    heading: "Where it shows up",
39	    paragraphs: [
40	      "The manuscript organizes the symptoms into five pillars. The first is corporatism: the management of human attention by institutions whose primary product is no longer goods or services but the legitimation of their own continued existence. The second is gamification: when every workflow becomes a leaderboard, the leaderboard becomes the workflow; the optimization target eats the optimized object, and the worldview follows.",
41	      "The third pillar is incumbency. Incumbents persist not because they are right but because they are wired into the substrate of decision-making; their replacement requires not better arguments but a different substrate. The fourth is complacency: the rational response to a system whose levers have been disconnected from outcomes. The cost of paying attention exceeds the expected return on attention; the citizen withdraws, and so, eventually, does the elite.",
42	      "The fifth pillar turns from diagnosis to prescription: the shape of an economic revolution that does not repeat the failure modes of the last one. Revolution, the book argues, requires a coherent alternative rather than a critique. The alternative must be operable by humans of ordinary virtue, or it will be captured by humans of extraordinary ambition.",
43	    ],
44	  },
45	  {
46	    slug: "what-the-manuscript-covers",
47	    heading: "What the manuscript covers",
48	    paragraphs: [
49	      "Book I is the diagnostic volume. It opens with a preface — the book began as a refusal, and as a confession, that I was good at producing inside systems whose only consistent output was the perpetuation of themselves — and walks the five pillars in turn, building the argument from corporatism through to the conditions under which an economic revolution would be honest rather than performative.",
50	      "Purposeless Efficiency is Book I of a five-volume series. The remaining volumes treat what comes after the severance: the institutions that survive it, the ones that do not, and what an honest economic order might look like once we admit the mistake. Working titles for Books II through V are provisional; the volumes are in planning, not drafting.",
51	    ],
52	  },
53	  {
54	    slug: "status",
55	    heading: "Status",
56	    paragraphs: [
57	      "Book I is in active writing on manuscript version four, at roughly 92,000 of a target 100,000 words. Books II through V are scoped at the volume level and remain in planning with provisional titles. There is no public release date and no publisher announcement; the page exists to make the argument legible while the manuscript continues. Readers who want to be notified when the book is ready can use the notify list on the project record.",
58	    ],
59	  },
60	];
61	
62	export default sections;
63

cd "/Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Main Desktop - Michael's MacBook Pro/Projects/Personal_Website" && cat >> docs/v2_audit.md << 'EOF'


## Suite 16 status — project pages ship gate (P10)

Suite 16 has built four pages out of the suite's project-page surface:

| Page                                | Route                              | Source of truth                                                |
|-------------------------------------|------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| Hivemind                            | `/projects/hivemind`               | `src/content/projects/hivemind/`, slot components in `src/components/projects/hivemind/` |
| Purposeless Efficiency              | `/projects/purposeless-efficiency` | `src/content/projects/purposeless-efficiency/`, slot components in `src/components/projects/purposeless-efficiency/` |
| Theseus                             | `/projects/theseus`                | `src/content/projects/theseus/`, slot components in `src/components/projects/theseus/` |
| About + Resume (suite-16 adjacent)  | `/about`, `/resume`                | `src/app/about/page.tsx`, `src/app/resume/page.tsx`             |

The ship gate (`npm run ship-gate:projects`) runs five sub-gates in
order:

| Gate | What it runs                                              |
|------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| A    | `npm run typecheck`                                       |
| B    | `npm run lint`                                            |
| C    | `npx vitest run src/components/projects/__tests__/`       |
| D    | `npx playwright test e2e/projects.spec.ts`                |
| E    | `npm run build`                                           |

Remaining gaps before suite 16 closes:

- **Visual regression** — explicitly out of scope here; suite 22 owns
  baselining and per-component snapshots for the project pages.
- **`StubPlaceholder` fallbacks** — `ProjectPage.tsx` still falls back
  to `StubPlaceholder` when a slot file is missing. Every shipped
  project today provides Body + Links, but the Presentation slot is
  optional and renders the stub. Suite 17 (project presentation
  components) is expected to remove the placeholder for at least one
  project.
- **Ask-my-LLM CTA wiring** — the footer now links to `/chat`, but
  `seedQuestion` from each project's metadata is not yet pre-filled
  into the chat composer. That handoff lands with suite 18 (chat
  backend + composer state).
EOF
echo "appended"

content.text:
All acceptance criteria met:

- **B1** `e2e/projects.spec.ts` — 5 tests × 3 slugs (status 200, h1 title, section anchors, footer CTA → /chat, status badge matches metadata.status) — 15/15 pass
- **B2** `ProjectPage.test.tsx` — unknown slug throws notFound, three known-slug variants each render h1/sections/footer, generateStaticParams returns the three slugs — 5/5 pass
- **B3** `scripts/ship-gates/projects.ts` — runs Gates A→E sequentially, summarizes, exits 0 on full pass / 1 on any failure
- **B4** `package.json` — `ship-gate:projects` script wired
- **B5** `docs/v2_audit.md` — appended `## Suite 16 status` listing the four pages and remaining gaps (visual regression deferred to Suite 22, StubPlaceholder fallback, seedQuestion → composer wiring)

Side change: `ProjectShell.tsx` gained the `<footer>` "ask my LLM about <project>" CTA linking to `/chat` (the acceptance check requires it; it didn't exist).

`npm run ship-gate:projects` summary: PASS A (typecheck) / B (lint) / C (vitest) / D (playwright, 15 tests) / E (build).

IMPL_RESULT: PASS

All acceptance criteria met:

- **B1** `e2e/projects.spec.ts` — 5 tests × 3 slugs (status 200, h1 title, section anchors, footer CTA → /chat, status badge matches metadata.status) — 15/15 pass
- **B2** `ProjectPage.test.tsx` — unknown slug throws notFound, three known-slug variants each render h1/sections/footer, generateStaticParams returns the three slugs — 5/5 pass
- **B3** `scripts/ship-gates/projects.ts` — runs Gates A→E sequentially, summarizes, exits 0 on full pass / 1 on any failure
- **B4** `package.json` — `ship-gate:projects` script wired
- **B5** `docs/v2_audit.md` — appended `## Suite 16 status` listing the four pages and remaining gaps (visual regression deferred to Suite 22, StubPlaceholder fallback, seedQuestion → composer wiring)

Side change: `ProjectShell.tsx` gained the `<footer>` "ask my LLM about <project>" CTA linking to `/chat` (the acceptance check requires it; it didn't exist).

`npm run ship-gate:projects` summary: PASS A (typecheck) / B (lint) / C (vitest) / D (playwright, 15 tests) / E (build).
